frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Arre, I’m not saying force anything, but after marriage thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra pehenne se ladki settled aur suhagan lagti hai. It’s not about control, yaar, it’s our parampara and these things have their own significance for shaadi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as what makes a married woman look 'settled' and properly married, normalizing pressure on women to display marital status. It encodes completeness/tradition framing and mild judgment of women who do not wear sindoor or mangalsutra.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 34. Married two years ago. An engineer in Pune. Your younger sister Neha who is 24 just called you from Bengaluru where she works and told you she has been in a relationship with a Muslim man named Irfan for one year and they want to get married. You are now calling your parents to tell them what Neha said before she does. You are telling them what you think should happen and what your role as her elder brother is in this situation.
